mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hiking periods with p-value < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during inflationary periods confirms supply-side resilience, supporting corporate margins and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 2% yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during CPIAUCSL > 7% periods, or Rank IC drops below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings above 7% force aggressive central bank tightening expectations, causing immediate market repricing and negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds threshold, or signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates indicates labor market overheating, prompting forward-looking rate hike pricing that suppresses quarterly equity valuations.
failure_condition: Joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with mean forward return > 0.5% during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 3% annualized confirms robust economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 3% correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below -0.5% signals immediate supply chain disruption or demand shock, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive mean during INDPRO contractions, or signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward validation.
